activities_projects.title: Otter Drawing Activity
activities_projects.objective: Visualize Mijbil and his playful behavior.
steps: Draw Mijbil in his cage or while playing in water
Label parts of the otter and some actions
Add surroundings like toys or water if mentioned
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand Mijbil’s appearance and playful nature.

activities_projects.title: Character Traits List
activities_projects.objective: Identify qualities of Mijbil and his owners.
steps: List 5 qualities of Mijbil (e.g., playful, curious)
List 3 qualities of the owner or family
Give one example from the chapter for each trait
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will recognize character traits and provide textual support.

activities_projects.title: Sequence of Events
activities_projects.objective: Understand the story’s events in order.
steps: Write 6–8 main events in the story
Arrange them in chronological order
Read them aloud to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will recall events and organize them logically.

activities_projects.title: Diary Entry – Mijbil
steps: Imagine you are Mijbil for a day
Write a short diary entry describing your activities and feelings
Include interactions with humans and playful moments
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will enhance creative writing and perspective-taking skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and relevance to the story

activities_projects.title: New Vocabulary List
activities_projects.objective: Learn and apply new words from the chapter.
steps: Pick 10 new words from the chapter
Write their meanings
Use each word in a simple sentence
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will improve vocabulary and sentence construction skills.

activities_projects.title: Short Speech – My Pet Otter
steps: Prepare a 1–2 minute speech imagining you own Mijbil
Include his habits, playful nature, and care requirements
Present confidently in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop oral communication and empathy.

activities_projects.title: Moral of the Story Poster
activities_projects.objective: Identify and present the story’s message.
steps: Write the main moral (e.g., care and love for animals)
Add illustrations of Mijbil and key scenes
Present to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand story themes and express them visually.

activities_projects.title: Role Play – Mijbil’s Day
steps: Assign roles (Mijbil, owner, family)
Act out one playful or memorable scene from the story
Express emotions clearly
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop empathy, expression, and teamwork.

activities_projects.title: Observation & Description Task
steps: Describe Mijbil’s appearance, habits, and playful actions in 5–6 sentences
Include sensory details (sight, sound, touch)
Read aloud to class or share in small groups
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will improve observation and descriptive writing.

activities_projects.title: Question-Answer Activity
steps: Write 5 questions about the story (e.g., about Mijbil’s habits or incidents)
Answer them in full sentences
Pair up and exchange Q&A for discussion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will improve reading comprehension and critical thinking.
